checks.a_analysis: The output contains the logo, headings, agenda items, and meeting dates as presented in the source document. No fabricated body text, introductions, or conclusions were found. Added titles and summaries are permitted as per the instructions.
The URL 'http://it.nc.gov/gicc' and the physical address 'Education Building, Room 755, 301 N. Wilmington St., Raleigh, North Carolina' both match the source exactly.
All sections of the single-page document, including the header, main agenda table, and meeting dates/website footer, are present in the output.

The source table contains a header row followed by 11 agenda items (12 rows total). The JSON output contains 1 header row and 11 data rows, capturing all items including the multi-part Committee Reports (Item 8) within a single data row.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output captures all specific committee names, voters, and item descriptions. No significant text content was omitted from the sections.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the output matches the source closely. Items like 'AddressNC', 'NextGen911', and 'NC OneMap' are correctly preserved. Date formats and names are accurate.

checks.a_analysis: The content from the source document appears only once in the converted JSON. The document title in the metadata is a standard addition and does not constitute duplication of the body text. No paragraphs, list items, or table rows are repeated.

The heading hierarchy in the JSON uses H2 for the main titles and H3 for the meeting dates section. Although H1 is not explicitly used in the elements, the transition from H2 to H3 is logical and follows a sequential order without skipping levels. This is an acceptable normalization for document structure.

The table structure accurately reflects the 3-column layout of the source document. The header row is correctly identified. For complex items like Agenda Item 8, the conversion maintains the row integrity by placing the multi-line list content into a single cell, which preserves the overall table structure even if sub-row relationships are flattened into text.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the original agenda: logo, titles, meeting logistics, agenda table, and finally the upcoming meeting dates and website. This matches the intended order of the source PDF.

The source document uses a numbered list for the agenda and bulleted lists within item 8. The agenda numbers are correctly preserved as text within the table rows. The bulleted items in section 8 are rendered as text with bullet markers within table cells, which preserves their meaning and structure effectively within the table context.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The JSON contains 3 headings, while the rendered HTML contains 5. The difference of 2 headings is below the threshold of 3 required to fail this check. The discrepancy is likely due to standard accessibility additions by the renderer such as 'Table of Contents' or 'Document Title'.

The source document contains several clickable text links indicated by blue, underlined text (e.g., 'Minutes', 'Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)', and 'LGC Bylaws Revised'). These links are missing from the JSON output, where the text is rendered as plain text without hyperlinks. Only the website URL at the bottom of the document was captured as a link.
checks.b_evidence_source: Minutes (in Item 3); Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary) (in Item 8); LGC Bylaws Revised (in Item 8)
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding text is plain text with no 'href' attribute in the JSON elements.

checks.a_analysis: The overall visual organization of the document is maintained in the HTML conversion. The header information, the main agenda table, and the footer meeting dates are all presented in the same relative order and structure as the source PDF. While there are styling differences (fonts, colors, etc.) and some content duplication in the headers, the fundamental layout of the information remains consistent with the source's intent.

In the agenda table, row 8 (Committee Reports) is misrendered. In the source PDF, this row contains a list of sub-items where committees in the middle column are horizontally aligned with specific names in the right column (e.g., 'Local Government' aligns with 'Kathryn Clifton'). In the HTML output, these columns are rendered as single blocks of text within their respective cells, breaking the visual alignment and mapping between committees and their representatives. This makes the table data significantly harder to interpret correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: Row 8 of the table shows committee names (e.g., 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee') aligned horizontally with individual names (e.g., 'Ryan Draughn').
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML table, Row 8 contains all committee names in one cell and all member names in another cell, causing 'Ryan Draughn' to appear at the top of its cell across from '8. Committee Reports' instead of its corresponding committee.

checks.a_analysis: The primary meaningful image from the source PDF, the NCGICC logo, is correctly rendered in the HTML output.

The main document title 'North Carolina GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION COORDINATING COUNCIL' and the heading 'AGENDA' are duplicated at the start of the document. They appear first as a combined text block at the very top of the page and then again separately following the 'Meeting Agenda' heading and logo.
checks.b_evidence_source: The title 'North Carolina GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION COORDINATING COUNCIL' and 'AGENDA' appear once in the header section.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text 'North Carolina GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION COORDINATING COUNCIL AGENDA' appears at the top of the page, then the title and 'AGENDA' appear again separately below the 'Meeting Agenda' heading.
checks.b_evidence_location: Top of the document, before and after the table of contents and logo.

checks.a_analysis: Beyond the table alignment and header duplication issues noted in other checks, the overall visual quality is acceptable. The text is legible, and the structural integrity of the document's sections is preserved.
